title: Implantable medicament delivery system
abstract: A flexible implantable contraceptive disc device is disclosed which can be inserted inside the female body, such as inside the uterus, is disclosed. The circular device can be bent and inserted into a laparoscopy tube which allows it to be delivered to the appropriate location inside a female subject. The disc has three layers, a central core silicon reservoir containing the active ingredient, such as a contraceptive progesterone, which is sandwiched and encased by an upper and lower porous silicon casings. The casings are porous to the contraceptive, allowing controlled release over a prolonged period of time. The lower casing has a series of micro-hooks around the discs circumference, allowing the disc device to be attached to a desired tissue by rotating the disc such that the hooks engage with the tissue. Such a device is easier to insert, and to remove, than the rod-like rigid plastic T-shape IUDs which are considerably larger, also more expensive to manufacture.
claims: Claims 1. An implantable medicament delivery system (implant) comprising: (a) a medicament reservoir; and (b) a porous casing wherein the system has at least two configurations, at least one of which is substantially flat or planar. 2. An implant according to claim 1, wherein the substantially flat or planar configuration has sufficient structural integrity to maintain said configuration. 3. An implant according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the substantially flat or planar configuration is square, rectangular or substantially disc-like. 4. An implant according to any one of the preceding claims, which comprises two or three layers, wherein the reservoir is distinct from the casing and the implant comprises one or two casing layers. 5. An implant according to claim 4, wherein the reservoir is at least partially enclosed by the casing. 6. An implant according to claim 4 or 5, wherein there is an upper casing and a lower casing. 7. An implant according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the casing is a silicon matrix. 8. An implant according to any one of the preceding claims, which is wholly flexible, and has no rigid parts or components. 9. An implant according to any one of the preceding claims, which in one configuration fits inside a laparoscopy tube of a diameter from about 5mm. 10. An implant according to any one of the preceding claims, which in one configuration fits inside a laparoscopy tube of a diameter to about 10mm. 11. An implant according to any one of the preceding claims, which further comprises tissue attachment means configured to attach the casing to a tissue. 12. An implant according to claim 11, wherein there are a plurality of attachment means such that a surface or face of the implant can be in contact with the tissue. 13. An implant according to claim 11 or 12, wherein the attachment means comprises micro-hooks. 14. An implant according to claim 13, wherein the micro-hooks are disposed around or near the edges of the porous casing. 15. An implant according to claim 13 or 14, wherein each of the micro-hooks has a length projecting from the porous casing and a tissue engaging projection at its distal end. 16. An implant according to any of claims 13 to 15, wherein the outer diameter of the micro-hooks is around 0.18mm to around 0.36mm. 17. An implant according to any of claims 13 to 16, wherein the tissue engaging projection at the distal end of the micro-hooks further comprises a bevel to pierce the tissue in use. 18. An implant according to claim 15, wherein the length projecting from the porous casing is around 0.5mm to around 1.5mm; and the length of the tissue engaging projection is around 0.25mm to 0.75mm. 19. An implant according to claim 18, wherein the angle formed between the length of the micro-hook projecting from the porous casing and the tissue engaging projection is between around 90Â° to around 135Â°. 20. An implant according to any of claims 13 to 19, wherein the micro-hooks are formed from stainless steel and/or carbon steel. 21. An implant according to any one of the preceding claims, which is a contraceptive device or wherein the medicament has progestogenic activity. 22. An implant according to any one of claims 1 to 21 for use as a medicament or for use in medicine. 23. An implant according to any one of claims 1 to 21 for use in a method of treatment of the human or animal body. 24. A method of contraception using an implant as defined in any one of claims 1 to 21. 25. A method of treatment of the human or animal body, the method comprising implanting an implant according to any one of claims 1 to 21 into a human or animal body. 26. The method of claim 25, which is a method of contraception and the human or animal is female. 27. Use of an implant according to any one of claims 1 to 21 in the manufacture of a medicament. 28. Use according to claim 27, wherein the medicament is a contraceptive.
